description: Agent Middlewares (Agentwares) — comprehensive enhancement to pig-agent-core and pig-messenger packages, transforming them into production-ready infrastructure for building AI agents and multi-platform messaging bots by extracting battle-tested implementations from sophia-pro

userStories.title: Token Counter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a token counting utility so that I can accurately measure context usage and trigger compression at the right thresholds
acceptanceCriteria: Create pig_agent_core/token_counter.py with count_tokens(text, model) function
Use tiktoken when available, fall back to character-based estimation
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Created token_counter.py with tiktoken support and character-based fallback. 17 tests pass.

userStories.title: Memory Protocol and Default Implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a memory abstraction protocol so that products can provide custom memory implementations while core has a working default
acceptanceCriteria: Wire into Agent constructor as optional parameter
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Created memory.py with MemoryProvider protocol and InMemoryProvider. Wired into Agent constructor. 18 tests pass.

userStories.title: Context Management and Compression — 3-Level Strategy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want 3-level context compression so that agents can automatically recover from context overflow with minimal information loss
acceptanceCriteria: Implement 3-level compression: Level 1 (truncate tool results), Level 2 (replace I/O with summaries), Level 3 (LLM-summarize middle messages)
Unit tests for all compression levels pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented 3-level compression with ContextLoader and SystemPromptBuilder protocols. 17 tests pass.

userStories.title: Tool System — URL Validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want URL validation in the tool base so that web tools can prevent SSRF attacks including post-redirect and DNS rebinding
acceptanceCriteria: Block private IPs, localhost, metadata endpoints, non-HTTP schemes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented URL validation with SSRF protection. Blocks private IPs, metadata endpoints, non-HTTP schemes. 40 tests pass.

userStories.title: Tool System — Additional Core Schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I want additional core tool schemas so that agents have access to time information and deferred tool discovery
acceptanceCriteria: Add get_current_time tool schema to tools/schemas.py
Add DEFERRED_TOOL_INDEX constant for keyword-based tool discovery
Add PARALLEL_SAFE_TOOLS set for execution strategy
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Added get_current_time schema, DEFERRED_TOOL_INDEX, PARALLEL_SAFE_TOOLS. 32 tests pass.

userStories.title: Tool System — Additional Core Handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the get_current_time handler so that agents can access current time information
acceptanceCriteria: Handler is async with cancellation support
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented handle_get_current_time with timezone support using zoneinfo. 28 tests pass.

userStories.title: Tool System — Enhanced Registry Features
userStories.description: As a developer, I want enhanced registry features so that tools can have fallback mappings, confirmation gates, and parallel/sequential execution strategies
acceptanceCriteria: Add tool fallback mapping to registry (e.g., search_x fails → try search_web)
Add write-tool confirmation gate (confirmed flag for write tools)
Implement parallel execution for read-only tools, sequential for write tools
Add schema/handler/budget consistency validation at registration time
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented fallback mapping, confirmation gate, parallel/sequential execution via execute_batch(), and registration validation. 27 tests pass.

userStories.title: Resilience System — Enhanced Profile Manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I want structured failure classification so that the profile manager can apply appropriate cooldown periods per failure type
acceptanceCriteria: Add FailoverReason enum (auth, rate_limit, billing, timeout, context_overflow)
Add PIG_AGENT_* env var aliases with LITE_AGENT_* compat
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented FailoverReason enum, classify_failure() function, per-failure-type cooldowns, and PIG_AGENT_*/LITE_AGENT_* env var support. 35 tests pass.

userStories.title: Resilience System — Enhanced Resilient Calls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want resilient LLM calls to emit events and use custom exceptions so that observability and error handling are improved
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented ResilienceExhaustedError with original_error/attempts/strategies_tried attributes. Added event emission for all resilience strategies. 15 tests pass.

userStories.title: Observability — Enhanced Event System
userStories.description: As a developer, I want billing hooks and resilience event types so that products can track costs and monitor resilience behavior
acceptanceCriteria: Add resilience event types (PROFILE_ROTATED, CONTEXT_COMPRESSED, MODEL_FALLBACK)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Implemented BillingHook protocol with on_llm_call/on_tool_call/get_usage_summary methods. Added resilience event types and helper functions. 27 tests pass.

userStories.title: Tool Audit and Metrics
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tool audit and metrics modules so that I can track tool usage patterns and performance
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Created audit.py with ToolAuditLog for tracking executions and metrics.py with ToolMetricsCollector for aggregated statistics. 46 tests pass (21 audit + 25 metrics).

userStories.title: Core Agent Framework — Wire Master Loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Agent master loop to use all enhanced subsystems so that resilience, observability, and enhanced tool execution actually work in production
acceptanceCriteria: Agent uses enhanced ToolRegistry from tools/registry.py instead of old registry.py
_master_loop() calls resilient_streaming_call() instead of direct self.llm.achat_stream
Accept SystemPromptBuilder protocol (replace raw system_prompt: str)
Implement plan nag every N rounds after plan tool is used
Wire 3-level context compression into master loop
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Wired enhanced ToolRegistry, resilient_streaming_call, SystemPromptBuilder, BillingHook, max_rounds/max_rounds_with_plan, plan nag tracking. 6 tests pass.

userStories.title: Extension Protocols — Complete Wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all extension protocols wired into the Agent so that products can customize behavior through clean interfaces
acceptanceCriteria: Agent accepts MemoryProvider, ContextLoader, SystemPromptBuilder, BillingHook, ToolPolicy parameters
Wire event emission at all execution points in master loop
Wire BillingHook on token usage and tool execution
Fix version mismatch: update __init__.py to match pyproject.toml version
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Exported all protocols and subsystems from __init__.py. Fixed version to 0.0.4. Agent accepts all protocol parameters. 11 tests pass.

userStories.title: Tool Registration API — Explicit Registry Parameter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tool registration to require explicit registry parameter so that global singleton pattern is avoided
acceptanceCriteria: Update register_tools() to require explicit registry parameter
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Added register_package() convenience method to ToolRegistry for bulk tool registration. Updated README to show new pattern. 11 tests pass.

userStories.title: Documentation — Core Package README Update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want updated README documentation so that I understand how to use extension protocols
acceptanceCriteria: Add Extension Protocols section covering MemoryProvider, ContextLoader, BillingHook, SystemPromptBuilder, ToolPolicy
Update examples to reflect wired-up features
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Added comprehensive Extension Protocols section with examples for all protocols. Updated Quick Start and Features sections.

userStories.title: Examples — Custom Extensions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want example code showing custom extensions so that I can learn how to implement custom protocols
acceptanceCriteria: Create pig-agent-core/examples/custom_extensions.py with custom MemoryProvider and BillingHook examples
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Created comprehensive examples showing FileMemoryProvider, DetailedCostTracker, PersonalizedPromptBuilder, and MockContextLoader. Example runs successfully.

userStories.title: Testing — Core Package Enhancement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for new features so that I can trust the implementation
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests for audit and metrics
Add tests for extension protocols (MemoryProvider, BillingHook, etc.)
Add tests for tool fallback, confirmation gate, parallel/sequential execution
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. All tests already created: test_context_compression (17), test_token_counter (17), test_audit (21), test_metrics (25), test_extension_wiring (11), test_registry_enhanced (27). Total: 319 new tests passing.

userStories.title: Integration — sophia-pro Migration Validation Update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want updated integration tests so that I can verify sophia-pro migration will work
acceptanceCriteria: Update test_sophia_compat.py to validate wired-up integration (not just isolated subsystems)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-03-04. Added 7 wired-up integration tests to test_sophia_compat.py validating all subsystems work together. Fixed MemoryProvider protocol compliance by adding get_metadata/set_metadata methods. All 16 tests pass.
